_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L4 'Conscript Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L5 'Sergeant' variant (Tier 2 mid).
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Sergeant beat = 5 visible adds (steel-faced shield replacing wooden plank face, steel partial breastplate covering upper chest only with livery visible BELOW as a chest band, steel vambraces replacing leather bracers on both forearms, mace head reinforcement plate wrapping the widest part of the mace head, steel bevor/half-collar covering throat). Within-tier chained input strategy (Rule 2): two inputs = L4 composite + L1_Base scale anchor. CRITICAL livery preservation language reminding the model that livery transitions to a CHEST BAND between breastplate (above) and belt (below) — does NOT disappear. Audit note: L4 keeper has only ~2 of 3 iron mace banding rings visible (soft fail at L4 lock); L5 prompt re-asserts the iron banding rings are present on the wooden mace head.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — REMOVED L5 mace head reinforcement plate (surface ornament with low thumbnail read; L4 iron banding rings already give the mace its banded read per Detail Economy rule). Replaced with FULL BICEP-TO-WRIST scale-mail extension per AD 'the chainmail being sleeveless feels odd. For the later levels, I would extend it to fully cover the arms.' L5 scale-mail sleeves now extend down past the elbow to the wrist, with the new vambraces sitting ON TOP at the forearm — no exposed bicep gap from L5 onward. Updated CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION: removed knife-sheath line (dropped per AD); added L3 steel skullcap line + L4 iron toe-cap-strip line. Negatives updated to lock out exposed-bicep returns and to drop the chest-strap rivet plate / mace reinforcement plate.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v5 (2026-05-07): scrub stale L2 rim-rivet PRESERVE/negative refs — L2 IRON RIM RIVETS positive add was dropped; downstream shield-rim language no longer references L2 rivets.
v6 (2026-05-07): surgical scrub of remaining stale rim-stud language across PRESERVE blocks.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L4/composite/infantry_L4_v1_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Conscript Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every + + feature shown in the input image: (a) QUILTED BROWN LEATHER SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms — the existing quilted leather under-tunic stays UNDER the existing scale-mail sleeves; (b) WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle; (c) BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap and brass stud on anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT) — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath, NO second pouch; (d) STEEL SKULLCAP visible UNDER the mail coif (small cool-gray steel dome at the very top of the head + thin steel rim band visible across the brow line just inside the coif's facial opening + two iron rivets at the temples) — this skullcap remains visible at under the coif; (e) TWO STEEL PAULDRON CAPS — one on each shoulder, with three or four dark-iron rivets along each pauldron's lower seam; (f) LEATHER GRIP CORD wrapping the upper third of the mace haft; (g) FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS radiating from the central steel boss in a cross pattern on the wooden shield face; (h) SCALE-MAIL SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms (chunky overlapping rounded scales matching the mail coif) — the new step EXTENDS these sleeves down to the wrist, see Edit Goal item below; (i) IRON BANDING RINGS on the wooden mace head (between and around the spike rows — at these rings stay PRESENT on the wooden mace head, do not remove them); (j) CONTINUOUS DARK-IRON RIM BAND wrapping the entire shield perimeter; (k) IRON TOE-CAP STRIP on the front of each boot (first boot armor — preserved; ankle plates arrive at this level, full sabatons at this level). DO NOT revert any of these features. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the mid Tier 2 step from Conscript Sergeant to Sergeant. The unit moves from leather/scale-sleeve kit to a partially-plated upper body while keeping the red+mustard split livery visible AS A CHEST BAND between the new partial breastplate (above) and the existing leather belt (below). (1) SCALE-MAIL SLEEVES EXTEND TO FULL BICEP-TO-WRIST COVERAGE — the existing short scale-mail sleeves (mid-bicep) now EXTEND DOWNWARD past the elbow all the way to the wrist, fully covering both arms in chunky overlapping rounded scale plates (same scale spec as the mail coif: chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in cool silver-gray steel, NOT chainmail rings, NOT fine ring mesh, NOT fish-scale fine texture). The quilted leather under-tunic at the upper arm is now hidden beneath the extended scale mail — only a thin band of warm-brown leather may remain briefly visible at the inner armpit. The new steel vambraces (per Edit Goal item 4) sit ON TOP of the scale-mail sleeves at the forearm, wrapping wrist to mid-forearm; the scale mail continues UNDER the vambraces from elbow to wrist. From onward there is NO exposed bare-skin bicep gap, NO bare elbow, NO bare upper-forearm — the entire arm from shoulder seam to wrist is covered (scale mail from shoulder-to-wrist with vambraces overlaying the wrist-to-mid-forearm section). (2) STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD — replace the WOODEN PLANK FACE of the round shield with a smooth painted-steel skin in cool silver-gray with faint vertical brushed-steel highlights running across the face. The wooden plank grain is FULLY REPLACED — no wood visible across the shield face. The existing four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss remain visible as raised dark-iron features on top of the new steel face; the existing continuous iron rim band remains the central cool-gray steel boss is now slightly larger and more domed than at this level (~3.5 inches up from 3 inches). The shield silhouette stays the same round shape; only the face material changes from wood to steel. (3) STEEL PARTIAL BREASTPLATE COVERING ONLY THE UPPER CHEST — add a smooth cool silver-gray steel breastplate piece covering ONLY the upper chest from collarbone down to roughly the bottom of the sternum (covering approximately the top half of the chest area). The breastplate has a slight medial ridge running vertically down the center. The lower edge of the breastplate is a clean horizontal line slightly above the level of the existing chest-strap rivet plate. CRITICAL: the LIVERY TUNIC remains FULLY VISIBLE BELOW the breastplate — the red+mustard split tunic now reads as a CHEST BAND between the breastplate's lower edge (above) and the wide leather belt (below). The livery band is approximately 3 inches tall and visibly shows BOTH the viewer-LEFT solid crimson-red half AND the viewer-RIGHT mustard-yellow half with the vertical crimson-red center stripe. Below the belt the livery tunic skirt also remains fully visible reaching mid-thigh. The chest-strap rivet plate is now incorporated into / hidden by the new breastplate at the upper chest. (4) STEEL VAMBRACES REPLACING THE LEATHER FOREARM BRACERS — replace the two existing brown leather forearm bracers with cool silver-gray polished steel vambraces wrapping wrist to mid-forearm on both arms. Each vambrace is a single piece of curved steel plate with two visible dark-iron rivets at each seam edge. The leather bracers are FULLY GONE — no warm-brown leather visible on either forearm. The vambraces sit ON TOP of the new full-arm scale-mail sleeves (per Edit Goal item 1) — the scale mail continues UNDER the vambraces from elbow to wrist with no exposed gap. The hands remain bare warm peachy-tan skin (anatomical-RIGHT hand grips the mace haft; anatomical-LEFT hand and forearm hidden behind the shield). (5) STEEL BEVOR / HALF-COLLAR — add a short cool silver-gray steel half-collar plate covering the front of the throat from the collarbone up to the lower jaw line, sitting over the front of the mail coif's collar drape. The bevor curves with the neck (concave shape facing the viewer) and adds visible volume at the upper chest silhouette. Top edge of the bevor reaches the lower lip of the beard; bottom edge sits just above the new breastplate's collarbone line. The bevor connects visually with the breastplate but is a separate piece (a thin shadow line is visible at the seam). The mail coif's neck-back drape remains visible behind the bevor. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with single bright catchlight; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin with rosy blush; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks (now framed at the bottom by the new bevor; beard remains visible above the bevor's top edge) — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild; his dark mustache; his dark-brown short hair as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front of the mail coif; the chunky scale-mail coif covering scalp, ears, and back-of-neck drape (front-of-throat now covered by the new bevor; the rest of the coif drape remains); the LIVERY TUNIC — viewer-LEFT solid crimson-red half, viewer-RIGHT mustard-yellow half with vertical crimson-red center stripe — now reading as a CHEST BAND between breastplate and belt PLUS continuing as the visible skirt below the belt; the wide warm-brown leather belt with square dark-iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath, NO second pouch; the existing steel skullcap visible under the mail coif (small cool-gray dome at the top + thin steel rim band at the brow line + iron rivets at temples); the existing iron toe-cap strip on the front of each boot; the two steel pauldron caps; the leather grip cord on the upper mace haft; the existing iron banding rings on the wooden mace head; the spiked wooden mace held in the anatomical-RIGHT hand — wooden haft, wooden bulbous mace head with iron banding, six iron spikes, mace head dangling at hip height angled down toward viewer-LEFT; the round shield held in anatomical-LEFT arm — now steel-faced (per Edit Goal item 1) but same round shape, same iron cross strap-bands, same iron rim band, same boss; the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers; the warm-brown leather boots with darker folded ankle cuff. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent ~120 degrees, hand grips the upper haft, mace head dangling at hip height angled down. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, forearm angled across the body, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height with face presenting toward viewer at slight three-quarter angle. Calm guard stance — both arms static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall), approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide). TOP of mail coif at ~16.6% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of boots at ~16.6% from bottom of canvas. Character vertically centered. ~18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, ~20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT remove the LIVERY tunic — the red+mustard split MUST remain visibly present as a CHEST BAND between the partial breastplate and the leather belt AND continuing as the visible skirt below the belt; DO NOT cover the entire chest with the breastplate — the breastplate covers ONLY the upper chest from collarbone to roughly the bottom of the sternum at this level; DO NOT extend the breastplate down to the belt or to the abdomen — full breastplate extension arrives at this level; DO NOT shrink the livery band into a thin strip narrower than ~3 inches; DO NOT replace the livery with a body coat, gambeson, surcoat, or any cloth other than the existing red+mustard split; DO NOT add tassets, fauld plates, hip plates, or any waist / hip armor — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add steel sabatons, greaves, leg armor — sabatons arrive at this level; DO NOT add a helm, sallet, kettle helm, or any structural addition to the head — head covering remains the existing mail coif (introduces the open-face sallet); DO NOT add a closed visor, half-visor, or any face cover — face remains fully visible; DO NOT add scale-mail body coat or extend the scale mail upward to cover the chest under the breastplate — the chest under the breastplate is the existing livery; DO NOT leave any exposed bare-skin gap on the bicep, elbow, or upper forearm at this level — the new full bicep-to-wrist scale-mail sleeves cover the entire arm from shoulder seam to wrist, with the steel vambraces sitting on top at the forearm; DO NOT keep the scale-mail sleeves at mid-bicep length — they MUST extend down past the elbow to the wrist at this level; DO NOT add a steel chest-strap rivet plate or central chest disc under or over the new partial breastplate (the existing chest-strap rivet plate was removed 2026-05-07); DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, scabbard, or any second belt accessory — the existing hip pouch is the ONLY belt accessory; DO NOT add a steel reinforcement plate or extra band wrapping the wooden mace head between the spike rows (removed at 2026-05-07; the existing iron banding rings already give the mace its banded read); DO NOT add a second mace, second shield, second weapon; DO NOT replace the wooden mace head with a flanged steel head — the wooden head + spikes + iron banding + new reinforcement plate is the existing mace; flanged steel arrives at this level; DO NOT change the iron spike count or arrangement on the mace head; DO NOT add iron banding to the haft — that arrives at this level; DO NOT replace or remove the leather grip cord on the upper haft; DO NOT change the round shield to a heater shield, kite shield, tower shield, square shield, or any non-round shape; DO NOT add embossed sun-burst boss or detailed boss pattern — that arrives at this level; DO NOT change the boss color to gold or add any gold to the shield; DO NOT add gauntlets — gauntlets arrive at this level; DO NOT make the steel vambraces extend into gauntlets — vambraces stop at the wrist, hands remain bare; DO NOT make the pauldrons layered multi-disc — at each pauldron is a single rounded steel cap (slightly larger than the existing pauldron disc visible in the input image); layered 2-disc pauldrons arrive at this level; DO NOT add gold trim, gold rivets, gold band, gold buckle, gold accents, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT add horns, crests, plumes, or vertical helm extensions; DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard; DO NOT change the hair, the coif color, the trouser color, or the boot color; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
